attempts.strategy: Use TypeCat.ofHom to wrap the quotMap function; build helpers fiberMap, quotMap with QuotientGroup.lift
attempts.code_tried:
noncomputable def PicardFunctor (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u where
  obj S := Pic (Limits.pullback C.hom S.unop.hom) ⧸
    (Pic.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd C.hom S.unop.hom)).range
  map {_ _} f := TypeCat.ofHom fun x ↦ PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop x
  map_id _ := by
    apply TypeCat.Hom.ext; apply TypeCat.Fun.ext; funext x
    change PicardFunctor.quotMap C (𝟙 _) x = x
    simp
  map_comp f g := by
    apply TypeCat.Hom.ext; apply TypeCat.Fun.ext; funext x
    change PicardFunctor.quotMap C (f ≫ g).unop x =
      PicardFunctor.quotMap C g.unop (PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop x)
    rw [unop_comp, PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp]; rfl
attempts.insight: Helpers fiberMap (the induced map on fiber products via pullback.map) and quotMap (the descent through QuotientGroup.lift, justified by the commutative square fiberMap ≫ pullback.snd = pullback.snd ≫ f.left) carry the structure. quotMap_id and quotMap_comp suffice for functoriality; pullback.hom_ext + the fiberMap_comp_fst / fiberMap_comp_snd simp lemmas are the robust route, since rewriting on pullback.map_comp directly fails due to path-dependent commuting-square proofs.

attempts.strategy: Discover QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff for the quotMap kernel-containment side condition
attempts.code_tried:
((QuotientGroup.mk' _).comp (Pic.pullback (fiberMap C f)))
  (by
    rintro x ⟨m, rfl⟩
    simp only [MonoidHom.mem_ker, ...]
    rw [QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff]
    refine ⟨Pic.pullback f.left m, ?_⟩
    rw [← MonoidHom.comp_apply ..., ← Pic.pullback_comp, ← fiberMap_comp_snd, Pic.pullback_comp, MonoidHom.comp_apply])

attempts.goal_before: ∀ x ∈ (Pic.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd C.hom S₁.hom)).range, (QuotientGroup.mk' _ ∘ Pic.pullback (fiberMap C f)) x = 1
attempts.insight: Mathlib uses `QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff` (loogle-discovered) for `↑x = 1 ↔ x ∈ N`. The descent through the quotient relies on the commutative square fiberMap_comp_snd, which is exactly the universal-property identity from pullback.lift_snd.

The iter-005 helpers fiberMap and quotMap are reusable as the entry points.
